# [Literature Review] Agents That Know Too Much: A Data-Centric Survey of Privacy in LLM Agents

This paper presents a comprehensive, data-centric survey of privacy in Large Language Model (LLM) agents, defining "data agents" as systems that query, retrieve, remember, and act upon sensitive data. It departs from traditional agent security literature—which often organizes by attack type—by framing privacy risks and governance through the lens of the data surfaces the agent interacts with during its multi-step execution.

### Core Methodology

The paper establishes a taxonomy centered on three interrelated components:

Data Surfaces: It identifies six primary environments where sensitive data resides: [...] Moonlight Logo

Governance Mechanisms: The survey categorizes defenses based on their efficacy and placement within the execution lifecycle:

### Technical Synthesis

A key contribution is the use of cross-mapping tables to relate surfaces to risks and governance. The analysis reveals two significant findings:

The survey concludes that the field is currently transitioning from treating LLMs as stateless chatbots to operating them as complex data systems. Consequently, it argues that future research must shift from simple output filtering toward architectural, path-based enforcement of privacy policies throughout the agentic workflow.

results.title: A Data-Centric Survey of Privacy in LLM Agents - arXiv
results.content:
We unify these lines under a single, data-centric view of LLM agents. Rather than
organize by attack type, as broad agent-security surveys
do (He et al., 2025; Gan et al., 2024; Qi et al., 2026), we organize
around the data itself: where it resides, the privacy risks it incurs, and how to
govern it. Concretely, we make the following contributions.

We motivate a data-centric view of agent privacy and explain why an LLM
agent that works with data, which we call a data agent, has privacy properties
different from those of stateless chatbots and generic tool-using agents
(Sections 1 and 2). [...] agent generates. This is the intersection of agent autonomy and enterprise data
governance, addressed by neither the agent-security surveys nor the
data-governance literature in isolation. [...] A data agent is an LLM agent whose task is to work with data: to query,
retrieve, transform, join, summarize, and act on it. The defining feature is
that data, rather than open-ended conversation, is both the input and the
object of the work. Recent treatments place data agents on a spectrum of
autonomy. Luo et al. (Luo et al., 2026a) propose levels from a natural
language interface that translates a single request into a query, through
agents that plan and execute multi-step analyses, to agents that proactively
decide what to analyze. Surveys of agents as data analysts and of data-science agents catalog the
capabilities along this spectrum (Tang et al., 2025; Rahman et al., 2025),
and a growing set of systems implements them: agents that perform end-to-end data

results.title: A Dataset-Centric Survey of LLM-Agents for Data Science
results.content:
Abstract: Large Language Model (LLM)-based agents are increasingly employed to automate data science workflows, from preprocessing and modeling to interpretation and decision-making. While recent surveys have explored their technical designs, little attention has been paid to how these agents adapt to datasets with different structures and domain requirements. This survey adopts a dataset-centric perspective, categorizing LLM-based data agents by the types of data they are designed for and evaluated on. We analyze key design choices—such as planning strategies, self-correction mechanisms, multi-agent collaboration, and tool integration—through the lens of structured, semi-structured, and unstructured data, as well as domain-specific applications. We introduce a hierarchical taxonomy that [...] back arrowGo to UIUC Spring 2025 CS598 LLM Agent Workshop homepage

## A Dataset-Centric Survey of LLM-Agents for Data Science

Download PDF

### UIUC Spring 2025 CS598 LLM Agent Workshop Submission13 Authors

17 Apr 2025 (modified: 18 Apr 2025)UIUC Spring 2025 CS598 LLM Agent Workshop SubmissionEveryoneRevisionsBibTeXCC BY 4.0

Keywords: Data Centric, LLM Agents, Survey Paper, Data Science [...] applications. We introduce a hierarchical taxonomy that connects agents’ capabilities in data management and analysis to their dataset contexts. Our analysis highlights current gaps in benchmark diversity and generalization, offering insights into the practical limitations of existing agents. We conclude by outlining future directions for designing and evaluating LLM-based data agents that are robust, adaptable, and dataset-aware.
